title: CSI ICH / DCH — Insert / Delete Character
summary: ICH (final '@') inserts Pn blank characters at the cursor, shifting the rest of the line right (characters pushed past the right margin are lost); DCH (final 'P') deletes Pn characters at the cursor, shifting the remainder of the line left with blanks appearing at the right margin. Both default Pn to 1 and act only on the current line.

attribution.note: xterm ctlseqs: CSI Ps @ = Insert Ps (Blank) Characters (ICH); CSI Ps P = Delete Ps Characters (DCH). Default Ps = 1.
ECMA-48 defines ICH (final '@') and DCH (final 'P') operating on the active line, default Pn 1.

frame.note: CSI + single Pn + final byte: '@' insert-char (0x40), 'P' delete-char (0x50). Pn defaults to 1; scoped to the current line.

terminator_detail.note: No string terminator; the CSI sequence ends at its final byte. Parameters Ps are decimal, ';' (0x3B) separated.

params.name: ICH — Insert Character (@)
params.meaning: CSI Pn @ inserts Pn blanks at the cursor; characters to the right shift right by Pn and any pushed past the right margin are lost. The cursor does not move.

params.name: DCH — Delete Character (P)
params.meaning: CSI Pn P deletes Pn characters at the cursor; the remainder of the line shifts left and blanks (current background) appear at the right margin.

gotchas: ICH/DCH operate within the single current line; they never affect other rows (use IL/DL for line-level edits).
Inserted/exposed cells take the current SGR background color.
Characters shifted past the right margin by ICH are discarded, not wrapped.
ICH does NOT move the cursor; the inserted blanks appear AT the cursor and the cursor stays put.
Distinct from ECH (CSI X) which erases in place WITHOUT shifting the rest of the line.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: ICH ('@') and DCH ('P') render as byte-exact single-parameter CSI sequences.
